Henri Rousseau

10 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Hikers climbing up to a Mountain Chalet
by Henri Rousseau
France · 1883–1893 · Black crayon
Mess Downstream, the Auteuil Quai
by Henri Rousseau
France · 1885 · Pen and black ink and brush and brown wash
Sawmill, Outskirts of Paris
by Henri Rousseau
France · 1888–1895 · Oil on canvas
The War
by Henri Rousseau
France, 19th century · 1894–1895 · lithograph
War
by Henri Rousseau
France · 1894 · Lithograph in black on orange wove paper
The Sleeping Gypsy
by Henri Rousseau
Henri Rousseau · 1897 · Museum of Modern Art
A dark-robed traveler sleeps beside her mandolin while a lion sniffs her face under a flat full moon. Rousseau, a self-taught customs clerk, offered to sell it to his hometown mayor for 200 francs; he was politely declined.
Outskirts of Paris
by Henri Rousseau
France, late 19th Century-early 20th Century · c. 1897–1905 · oil on fabric
Oak Branch
by Henri Rousseau
France · 1907–1908 · Pen and gray ink on cream wove paper
Fight Between a Tiger and a Buffalo
by Henri Rousseau
France, late 19th-early 20th Century · 1908 · oil on fabric
The Dream (Rousseau)
by Henri Rousseau
Henri Rousseau · 1910 · Museum of Modern Art
Rousseau's final completed work: a nude Yadwigha reclines on a Second Empire sofa that floats through a moonlit jungle of invented foliage, a snake charmer piping behind. He insisted the sofa's incongruity was perfectly explained by the word 'dream.'
←Attributed to Anne-Louis Girodet de Roucy-Trioson Théodore Rousseau→
